theory.canonical_statement: jectories correspond to cycles on a toroidal manifold. 2.0.165 ID 314: Extended Phase Space for MagnetoAcoustic–Kaluza–Klein Systems Type: Definition Formal Statement: The configuration manifold for coupled magnetoacoustic and compactified extradimensional modes is $M=m=0∞(TSO(3)× R3⊗HmKK)/GgaugeM$ = \prod {m=0}{\infty}(T*SO(3) \times \mathbb{R}ˆ3 \otimes $Hˆ{KK} m)/G {\text{gauge}}M=m=0∞(TSO(3)× R3⊗HmKK)/Ggauge,$ where HmKKHˆ{KK} mHmKK denotes the mmmth Kaluza–Klein sector Hilbert space and $Ggauge={:R3→U(1)∇2=0}G {\text{gauge}}$ = {\phi : \mathbb{R}ˆ3 \to $U(1),|,\nablaˆ2\phi=0}Ggauge={:R3→U(1)∇2=0}$ fixes gauge freedom. Interpretive Meaning: This extended phase space captures the degrees of freedom for magnetoacoustic fields coupled to a tower of Kaluza–Klein modes. It includes rotational and translational variables as well as gauge degrees of freedom and infinite KK sectors. Dependencies: ID 200 (implicit magnetoacous
theory.technical_interpretation: Definition of Ggauge is an algebraic definition, written Ggauge=:R3→U(1)∇2=0G gauge. It is an algebraic definition expressing the quantity directly in terms of the variables on its right-hand side. It is built from R, U, G, defining Ggauge. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
